description: ESM-IF inverse folding perplexity — how "surprised" the structure-conditioned language model is by the sequence. Lower = sequence fits structure better.
Guruprasad instability index. >40 = predicted unstable in vitro.
Grand Average of Hydropathy (Kyte-Doolittle). Positive = hydrophobic, negative = hydrophilic.
Predicted fraction of residues retaining structure at 40C.
Predicted fraction retaining structure at 45C.
Predicted fraction retaining structure at 50C.
Predicted fraction retaining structure at 55C.
Predicted fraction retaining structure at 60C.
Predicted fraction retaining structure at 65C.
Predicted fraction retaining structure at 70C.
Predicted fraction retaining structure at 75C.
Predicted fraction retaining structure at 80C.
Predicted thermophilicity class: 0=mesophilic, 1=undetermined, 2=thermophilic, 3=hyperthermophilic.
Whether the conserved catalytic Asp triad (YXDD motif) was found (1=yes, 0=no).
Distance (Angstroms) between 1st and 2nd catalytic Asp residues.
Distance (Angstroms) between 2nd and 3rd catalytic Asp residues.
RMSD (Angstroms) of the best-fit Asp triad geometry vs reference HIV-RT. Lower = more similar to known active RT.
Hydrophobic contacts per residue.
Salt bridges per residue.
Hydrogen bonds per residue.
Pocket hydrophobic contacts per pocket residue.
Pocket H-bonds per pocket residue.
Mean Kyte-Doolittle hydrophobicity across all residues.
Standard deviation of hydrophobicity — measures hydrophobic patterning.
Fraction of hydrophobic residues near the YXDD catalytic motif.
Mean hydrophobicity near the YXDD motif.
Whether a beta-hairpin near the active site was found (1=yes, 0=no).
Whether the Y residue of YXDD catalytic motif is in beta-strand (1=yes, 0=no). Canonical active geometry.
Whether the X residue of YXDD motif is in a turn (1=yes, 0=no).
Whether the first D of YXDD motif is in a turn (1=yes, 0=no).
Whether the second D of YXDD motif is in beta-strand (1=yes, 0=no). Canonical active geometry.
Best TM-score to any of 25+ reference RT structures (FoldSeek). Higher = more structurally similar.
Best lDDT score to any reference RT structure. Measures local distance accuracy.
Best fractional sequence identity to any reference from FoldSeek alignment.
Best TM-score to HIV-1 RT reference structures.
Best TM-score to MMLV RT reference structures (the gold-standard PE RT).
Best TM-score to MMLV prime editor complex structures.
Best TM-score to Retron RT reference structures.
Best TM-score to LTR retrotransposon RT reference structures.
Best TM-score to Group II Intron RT reference structures.
Best TM-score to Telomerase RT reference structures.
Evolutionary family classification (Retroviral, Retron, LTR_Retrotransposon, Group_II_Intron, CRISPR-associated, Other, Unclassified).
Protein sequence length in residues.
ESM-IF mean log-likelihood per residue. Higher (less negative) = better sequence-structure compatibility.
Net charge at pH 7.0 (positive charges minus negative charges).
CamSol intrinsic solubility score — positive = soluble, negative = aggregation-prone.
Ratio of alpha-helix to beta-sheet content (helix fraction / sheet fraction). Higher = more helical.
Standard deviation of hydrophobicity near the YXDD catalytic motif — measures hydrophobic patterning in the active site.
Number of resolved residues in the predicted 3D structure (may differ from seq_length due to disordered regions).
Solvent-accessible surface area per residue across the full structure (Å²/residue).
Fraction of apolar (hydrophobic) surface area relative to total SASA.
Mean relative solvent accessibility (0–1 scale) across all residues. Higher = more exposed.
Mean relative solvent accessibility of active-site pocket residues. Higher = more accessible catalytic site.
Average SASA of N-terminal active-site residues (Å²).
Percentage of residues in Ramachandran-favoured regions.
Percentage Ramachandran outliers (higher = worse structure quality).
Confidence score of the best beta-hairpin detection near the active site.
Amino acid sequence of the YXDD catalytic motif region (e.g., YADD, YMDD). NaN if motif not found.
Mean electrostatic surface potential (kT/e) across the entire protein surface. Negative = acidic surface.
Mean electrostatic surface potential of the fingers subdomain.
Mean electrostatic surface potential near conserved motifs 1 and 2 (palm domain catalytic residues).
Mean electrostatic surface potential within 3Å of the conserved LPQG/SP motif region.
Mean electrostatic surface potential within 5Å of the YXDD catalytic motif.
Mean electrostatic surface potential of the palm subdomain (catalytic core).
Mean electrostatic surface potential of the thumb subdomain (nucleic acid binding).
Mean electrostatic surface potential of the connection subdomain (links palm to RNase H domain).
